articles.title: Odisha govt orders uninterrupted water, power supply ahead of heatwave - Odisha TV
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Odisha
articles.full_text:
The Odisha government has stepped up preparations to tackle the summer heat, directing officials across departments to ensure an uninterrupted supply of drinking water and electricity throughout the season.
The directive was issued by Revenue and Disaster Management Minister Suresh Pujari during a high-level review meeting on heatwave preparedness. The meeting was attended by district collectors, municipal commissioners, revenue divisional commissioners, and senior officials from multiple departments.
Officials said the government has prioritised public awareness to minimise heat-related illnesses and fatalities. Arrangements have already been made to provide drinking water at key public locations such as markets, bus stands, and other crowded areas in both urban and rural regions.
Departments, including Housing and Urban Development, as well as Panchayati Raj and Drinking Water, have been instructed to deploy water tankers in areas facing shortages. Authorities have also been asked to repair defunct tube wells and ensure proper functioning of piped water supply systems.
On the healthcare front, the Health and Family Welfare Department has been directed to maintain sufficient stocks of essential medicines, oral rehydration solutions (ORS), and saline across hospitals and health centres. Special beds are also to be earmarked for patients suffering from heatstroke.
The minister further advised district administrations to adjust school timings in line with prevailing weather conditions and introduce “water bells” to ensure regular hydration breaks for students.
In addition, the Transport Department has been tasked with regulating passenger vehicle movement during peak heat hours and ensuring availability of drinking water and ORS for commuters.
Special measures are also being taken to safeguard animals. Departments such as Fisheries & Animal Resources Development and Forest, Environment & Climate Change have been asked to arrange water facilities for domestic animals, birds, and wildlife.

To support uninterrupted electricity during peak demand, the Energy Department has been directed to take necessary steps to prevent power disruptions.
As part of awareness efforts, posters and banners are being distributed across districts, while round-the-clock control rooms have been activated at both state and district levels to respond to emergencies during the heatwave period.
